reasoning: CVS shows strong upward momentum, having risen from $54 last year to $81.26 on the reference date (2025-10-26, using the Oct 24 close). The company raised its full-year earnings guidance twice in 2025, most recently in August, and analysts (Leerink) further boosted Q3 EPS estimates on October 16. This provides a positive backdrop for the October 29 earnings call, which will likely dictate the price on the first resolution date (Nov 02). While the stock is at a local peak, which increases the risk of a "sell the news" pullback, the prevailing trend and positive analyst revisions support a probability slightly above the base rate for an increase. As the forecast horizon extends, the predictive power of current momentum fades, so probabilities move closer to the historical upward drift baseline (approx. 0.52).
